name: Observe any n8n workflow and post rich error alerts to Slack

parameters.content:
## Overview

Drop the **Org21-Observer** node inline anywhere in a workflow and it will capture per-node metadata, items, timing, and errors — then POST the whole payload to the sub-flow below. The sub-flow filters for errors and posts a rich, contextual Slack alert.

## How to use

1. **Install** `n8n-nodes-org21` via **Settings → Community nodes**.
2. **Configure Slack credentials** on the *Post to Slack* node (no API keys live in this template).
3. **Activate the sub-flow** (right branch), copy its Webhook URL.
4. Paste that URL into the **Webhook URL** field of the *Org21-Observer* node.
5. Execute the main branch — any error on any upstream node will light up Slack with workflow name, failing node, timing, and the payload that caused the failure.

## Customize

- Swap the Manual Trigger + HTTP Request + Set for **your** real workflow. The Observer is drop-in.
- Swap Slack for Teams / Discord / email — keep the sub-flow, replace only the last node.
- Disable `Include Item Data` on the Observer if payloads contain PII.

## Org21-Observer

Sits **inline** (not at the end) — `Pass Through` is on, so your workflow keeps running normally. The Observer only fans the sniffed payload sideways to the sub-flow.

If the upstream HTTP Request fails, `Include Errors` captures the error object; otherwise the payload still lands with timing + metadata. The sub-flow decides whether to alert.

## Sub-flow

Receives every Observer POST. The `IF` filters for runs that carried a non-empty `errors` array — silent success executions don't alert.

**To swap Slack** for another channel: delete the *Post to Slack* node and drop in Microsoft Teams / Discord / Gmail. The formatted message from the *Format Alert* Set node works for any text channel.

assignments.name: formattedMessage
assignments.value:
=:rotating_light: *n8n workflow error*
Workflow: *{{ $json.body.metadata.workflowName }}*  (`{{ $json.body.metadata.workflowId }}`)
Execution: `{{ $json.body.metadata.executionId }}`
Failing node: *{{ $json.body.metadata.nodeName }}*
Duration: {{ $json.body.timing?.durationMs }} ms — items: {{ $json.body.timing?.itemCount }}
Error: ```{{ JSON.stringify($json.body.errors, null, 2).slice(0, 900) }}```
